title: ficus rigida var. bracteata
intro: Ficus rigida var. bracteata, also known as the Ficus bracteata, is a species of fig tree that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Ficus bracteata is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, Ficus bracteata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as a paste or salve, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
